item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:

Source: Piqsels
License: CC0
Title: N/A
Author: N/A
Release year: N/A

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: new image source

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh fuck that's coming up

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Loved it! Can't wait for SCP-5200!

item.title: Re: Stella
item.content:encoded:
10 points to Gryphindor!

The “Empty Box” title is a similar reference:)

item.title: Stella
item.content:encoded:
I can't believe that no-one has pointed out that the codename 'Stella' used in this article is referencing the codename the Atari-2600 developers gave to the system's 'Television Interface Adaptor' chip. That was nicknamed Stella. Because it was sexy.

Do I get geek-points now?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Quarter Junkies"

AHAHHAHAHAHHAHAHAHAHHAHAHAHAHHAHAHAHAH

Ahem. Er, sorry.

Uhh…

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm afraid of losing control of myself and my identity. Once I started reading about the details of the Arcadia effect, my stomach started churning and I felt my face drain of color. Very few articles successfully strike at my worst fears like that. Nice job.
+1

item.title: Spanish translation
item.content:encoded: Hi, Roget. I'm here to say that SCP-2600 can mindfuck be read in spanish
Yaaaaay

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feex

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
They found graphical images in SCP-1077? That's a fungus.

Thank you for the feex.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've updated this one again for flow and such, also moved it to Keter class since it did wipe out a whole Site on their own.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i think 2 is supposed to be static

item.title: Re: a thought...
item.content:encoded: That's part of what inspired the article, yeah.

item.title: a thought...
item.content:encoded: does anyone wants wonder if this has any connection to the Polybius project? I don't know but Portland Oregon+mind control+gaming systems SCREAMS Polybius

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While I really appreciate some parts of this, the amount of redaction is simply ridiculous.

M█-█████, really? Just put MK-Ultra, and don't needlessly redact things.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The Containment Attempt Summary is very awkward, and in general it's annoyingly obfuscatory (what the hell are "sta██a████n" and "█████ethical" supposed to be).

It's not clear how "efforts to block the initial creation of the targeted anomalous objects had failed, resulting in the initial creation of SCP-2600", or why the "exterior of Site-92 has been substantially reduced", these are very inscrutable additions.

The 'skin bumping up and down on a bus with the wheels and some boy's mother on a bus' hallucination is nonsensical in an almost good way. I LOLed at "It's just playing with itself in the memory it can access". +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've gone through and clarified this a bit, to the best of my memory. Check it out, if you get the chance.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is my favourite of the 2000 entries, and is one of the creepiest SCPs in my opinion, right up there with 087. I don't know why but it just hardcore freaks me out. Excellent!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Aaaand now I have that intro music stuck in my head for the next four weeks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like the MK-ULTRA and Polybius angle.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dunno if I read this before, but I like the note about Stella.

item.title: Missing face?
item.content:encoded:
Alright, so there's 8 TVs in the set, each one is displaying a face. Furthermore, each face is different, so there must be eight different faces on the TVs.

The first three listed are the kids from Oregon.
Then we have the white silhouette.
Then we get the ex-KGB guy.
And finally the two women who keep repeating the same phrases.

That's seven, so where is number eight?

item.title: Missed one!
item.content:encoded:
All SCP objects are to be transferred to the nearby Site-18, and SCP-2000 itself neutralized by Foundation staff.

That SCP-2000 should be SCP-2600.

That being said, I… I really have no idea what I just read? I mean, it seems interesting enough, but it didn't really capture my attention and I couldn't make sense of it. No vote from me for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1 for now, but I feel too confused to appreciate it to it's full potential, I think. Some of the description is confusing, and because a lot of this SCP is confusing I am never sure what is supposed to be confusing and what is supposed to be clear.

Faces displayed on SCP-2000 currently are:

How do we know, since it's current status is unknown?

All instances of SCP-2000 are connected to one Atari Video Computer System

Not clear whether each has it's own or whether they are all connected to the same. Not clear if each instance work individually or if all 8 work together.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm going to upvote this based on quality, but I can't for the life of me figure out what any of this has to do with sci-fi. You got some revisionist history, some repurposed artifacts from a bygone era, plenty of spy shenanigans, up to and including the requisite ████████, and an anomalous entity with atypical thinking patterns, somehow created through human experimentation. But you managed to implement all of this in such a way that it doesn't feel like science fiction to me at all. At best it feels like an episode of The X Files, more paranormal urban-fantasy than anything else. I don't know whether to give you credit for a novel take on sci-fi or complain that you just made a regular SCP.

I feel like it's a quality skip, though, so I'm upvoting based on quality.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

I like this, and hope to see it on the mainlist when the contest is over. There are some nitpicks, but I will save them for the mainlist.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well I like trying to figure out what redacted words are when I have enough letters and context to make it feasible. The only 13 letter word that starts with st and ends with n is strangulation. So that is ..um.. something that has lets say interesting implications on the article. I will edit and add more words as I figure them out.

1) in█████ Most likely "install". Also possible it is "ingress" or more creepily "inbreed".
2) st██ █c Unsure if this is meant to be one word or two. By context I believe it is one. Most likely "styptic" as that would jive with "strangulation"
3) d█████r Probably "deadlier".
4) con█ition███ Seems obvious that this is "conditioning"
5) 2%██████ Unsure of this word. As a interesting side note however, Dextrose and Maltodextrin together are used as workout supplements. Specifically a carbohydrate supplement. Not sure what it means without more context but it is interesting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
They're actually not random, everything in them is there for a reason.

And yeah that was a typo. Actually it wasn't a typo. He was only reported dead after the collapse of the Soviet Union.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It was almost a stunning and compelling story. Then instead of having any sort of answers or potential leads on the questions raised, it all seemed to have been replaced by a random madness log.

And how did someone's face appear on one of the screens of the object recovered in 1984 when the person died in 1991? Or is that a typo meant to be 1981?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Too much redaction and stuff just missing… and it relies much too hard on US history. After a lot of Googling, I think I've managed to catch all the references, but I'm still not sure I get it all. I'm afraid I know nothing at all about USA in the '80s!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I… don't understand it. Which is what I especially love about it. I'm sure that if I looked at the information hard enough I could come up with some insane headcannon. For now I'm happy with what's there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
After re-reading, I am on the brink of an upvote. I just don't know what the hell is going on, and the article sure isn't telling me.

I don't know what the relevance of Site-92 is, I can only just barely discern what the CIA is doing, "war simulation" really intrigued me but is so obscured by the ending that it almost seems to be an afterthought…even though it's probably underlying the whole story! I just don't know what war simulation has to do with anything, and I can come up with two dozen wildly different answers on my own that are vaguely substantiated by the article. And I don't know, the vagueness just does not work out for me. The blackboxing frustrated me more than anything.

I really really like the CIA war simulation conspiracy with Atari angle, I just don't get enough of that angle for it to work for me. It's all hidden behind blackboxes and flat-out withholding of information, so I can't upvote. I don't have the information to tie everything together into one cohesive piece.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm … also not sure what I'm reading. I'm intrigued, but uncertain. I'll have to re-read later. No vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So ET was not the only thing in that landfill. I like this, because I'm an Atari nut. Upvote hard!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I just couldn't make heads or tails of those documents.

(Goddammit, I have to stop using bbcode here.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah. If you read further, you'd see it was the folks in the screens scrambling the CIA docs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have to admit, I read the whole thing but I found it rather dull. And then the strangely redacted notes at the end ("d█ e █ █ v █ e █ l o █ped"?) just seem bizarre, and left me wondering what the point was.

Por Favor Motengo Se Alehajo Telas Quertas

I'm cringing, but I have to ask if this is meant to be in error.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thought about it and changed to an upvote because the originality carries it over any issues I have with sentence structure. I still recommend you take a look at my suggestions when you can.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've been out of town and won't be home till after midnight, could you refrain from a downvote until I have a chance to fix it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm going to upvote assuming you'll take a look at my suggestions later.

“Initially, while SCP-2000 was classified as Euclid, other, apparently failed, test materials were euthanized by the engineers of Mobile Task Force 43, ‘Quarter Junkies’.”

-Your use of commas makes the entire sentence confusing. I’m having a hard time understanding what you are trying to say. Dragging it into Word and autocorrecting it comes up with this, “Initially, while SCP-2000 was classified as Euclid, the engineers of Mobile Task Force 43, ‘Quarter Junkies’, euthanized other, apparently failed test materials.”

“The current status of SCP-2000 is unknown, as the anomaly it currently propagates makes future study difficult, to impossible.”

-There is an overuse of the root word “current”. You are also relating the present with the future in a way that doesn’t really work.
Rewording suggestion: “The status of SCP-2000 is currently unknown. The anomaly it propagates renders all methods of close examination with it to be non-viable.”

“Personnel are advised not to listen to any possible attempts by former SCP-2000 researchers to contact them, and to immediately contact their immediate supervisor should such a situation occur.”

-This is a bit of a run on sentence. Also you use the root word “immediate” twice in 4 words.
Rewording suggestion: “Personnel are not to listen to any possible attempts by former SCP-2000 researchers to contact them. Personnel are advised to contact their immediate supervisor should such a situation occur.”

"Research which led to the discovery of SCP-2000 was initially begun after an inquiry into the Unusual Incidents Unit"

-Autocorrected in Word to: “Research, which led to the discovery of SCP-2000, was initially begun after an inquiry into the Unusual Incidents Unit”

“Financial difficulties overtook Atari at this time, leading to its dissolution., and Foundation personnel were able to confiscate all of their materials relating to SCP-2000.”
-“dissolution.,” period-comma minor grammar mistake.

I’m having trouble figuring out exactly what it is, but I like the weirdness.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow, Atari building psycho devices. Makes me wonder what was really in that New Mexico landfill.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not sure what I'm reading… but I like it. Tentatively upvoting, may reconsider later.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No prob. Done. :3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If you could do that for me, I'd appreciate it. On my phone atm, it's hard to edit stuff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd say 'SCP-2000 and several non-anomalous television sets during testing', or the like in the caption then, just to clarify. Minor thang, feel free to ignore me. :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: >.> game theory may have been part of my research

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I thought some of those were speakers? (Apparently not)

And I agree with Fantem. Though I don't know exactly what's going on (Ihpkmn's spoiler helped a bunch), I can't deny that I liked the logs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's, uh, part of the test.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dammit, I was about to PM ya.

I rather like it; it has a good creep factor going on with those logs, and the way it's laid out makes me wonder if the black-boxes were intentionally redacted, or if it's some kind of corruption. One nitpick though: I counted 14 TV sets in the image; not 8 or less (I think that's a corner of a set in the top left-hand corner, so I counted that too).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Spoiler
But Hey, It's Just A Theory. A Conspiracy Theory!

So, this is Polybius meets MK-ULTRA? Methinks someone's been watching a bit too much Game Theory.

Anyway. I like it, especially the "Containment Attempt Summary", but I wish that the documents were a little less blackboxed- okay, I wish they were a lot less blackboxed. It's not enough to get me to neutral or downvote, but I'm a bit too thick to fill in the blanks for myself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not bad, but I just wasn't quite feeling it for a reason I can't quite put my finger on. Other than that, I do have a few nitpicks I'd like to point out:

And nits shall be picked
And the picking shall thus be ended

Face appears to have been removed,

The way this is worded, it makes it sound to me as though the subject's flesh composing his face was removed from his skull. I think something along the lines of "Video of the subject has been altered to obscure the face, leaving a white silhouette blah blah blah" would sound a bit better to me.

SCP-2000 is a set of 8 Cathode Ray Tube television sets,

All eight instances were deemed suitable for further study.

This is a bit contradictory. At the beginning you said that SCP-2000 was the designation for the eight units as a whole, while the second bit makes it sound as though each TV is an instance of SCP-2000. However you think best to fix that, it does mess with the article a bit as is.

Addendum 2000-C: [rest of log snipped for brevity]

I'd just like to point out that while it is acceptable in articles to leave spaces between blackboxed words, when you have huge lines blacked out like in this log, it wouldn't make any sense for the person going through and blacking out all these words to meticulously go through and make sure he/she left every space between the words untouched. While it's fine to do it otherwise, it would make it more believable (at least to me) if the blackboxing in the log was continuous with no spacing.

I'm neutral voting for now. That might change as I mull it over.
